members.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: shouldReactToEvent
members.description:
Returns `true` if
 - the ripple element is enabled
 - the pointer is primary for the input type
 - the pointer is the pointer that started the interaction, or will start
the interaction
 - the pointer is a touch, or the pointer state has the primary button
held, or the pointer is hovering

declarations.name: SelectOptionElement

members.name: value
members.description: The option's submitted value.

members.name: icon
members.description: Optional Material Symbol icon name shown before the label.

members.name: disabled
members.description: Disables the option so it cannot be selected.

members.name: selected
members.description: Reflects whether this option is currently selected. Set by wc-select.

members.name: keepOpen
members.description:
When true the menu stays open after selection (used for multi-select).
Set by wc-select.

members.name: filtered
members.description:
When true the option is hidden and excluded from keyboard navigation
because it does not match the current typeahead search query.
Set by wc-select.

members.name: item
members.description:
Returns the inner `wc-menu-item` element.
`wc-menu` discovers this via its `items` getter which checks `el.item`
as a `MenuItem` proxy, so keyboard navigation and activation work
without `wc-option` extending `MenuItem` directly.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The option's submitted value.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Optional Material Symbol icon name shown before the label.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the option so it cannot be selected.

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description: Reflects whether this option is currently selected. Set by wc-select.

attributes.name: keep-open
attributes.description:
When true the menu stays open after selection (used for multi-select).
Set by wc-select.

attributes.name: filtered
attributes.description:
When true the option is hidden and excluded from keyboard navigation
because it does not match the current typeahead search query.
Set by wc-select.

declarations.summary: A declarative option element for use inside wc-select.

declarations.name: Select

members.name: options
members.description:
Array of options to display in the dropdown.
Setting this property creates matching `<wc-option>` children automatically.

members.name: value
members.description: The selected value. For multi-select, a comma-separated list of values.

members.name: multiple
members.description: Enable multi-select mode.

members.name: search
members.description:
Enable typeahead search.
- `'contains'`: filters options client-side.
- `'managed'`: emits a `select-search` event for server-controlled filtering.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text shown when no value is selected.

members.name: label
members.description: Label displayed above the field.

members.name: clearable
members.description: Show a clear button in single-select mode when a value is selected.

members.name: variant
members.description: Visual variant of the field.

members.name: helperText
members.description: Helper text displayed below the field.

members.name: _noOptionsVisible
members.description: True when all options are filtered out by the current search query.

members.name: _syncProgrammaticOptions
members.description:
Reconciles the `options` property with auto-generated `<wc-option>` light-DOM
children (marked `data-generated`).  Declarative children placed by the
consumer are left untouched.

members.name: _syncOptionStates
members.description:
Pushes `selected`, `keepOpen`, and `filtered` state onto every `<wc-option>`
child element so each one can render itself correctly.

members.name: _getLabelForValue
members.description: Returns the display label for a given option value.

Visual and layout variant of the toolbar.
- `"docked"`: A full-width bar attached to the edge of the screen (default).
- `"floating"`: A detached pill-shaped toolbar that floats over content.

members.name: orientation
members.description:
Orientation of the toolbar content.
- `"horizontal"`: Items are laid out left to right (default).
- `"vertical"`: Items are stacked top to bottom. Primarily useful for the floating variant.

members.name: size
members.description:
Size of the docked toolbar.
- `"small"`: 56dp height (default).
- `"medium"`: 112dp height – suitable for a two-line header.
- `"large"`: 152dp height – suitable for a prominent header.

members.name: elevated
members.description: Whether the toolbar is visually elevated (adds a shadow).

declarations.name: dispatchActivationClick
declarations.description:
Dispatches a click event to the given element that triggers a native action,
but is not composed and therefore is not seen outside the element.

This is useful for responding to an external click event on the host element
that should trigger an internal action like a button click.

Note, a helper is provided because setting this up correctly is a bit tricky.
In particular, calling `click` on an element creates a composed event, which
is not desirable, and a manually dispatched event must specifically be a
`MouseEvent` to trigger a native action.

declarations.name: isActivationClick
declarations.description:
Returns true if the click event should trigger an activation behavior. The
behavior is defined by the element and is whatever it should do when
clicked.

Typically when an element needs to handle a click, the click is generated
from within the element and an event listener within the element implements
the needed behavior; however, it's possible to fire a click directly
at the element that the element should handle. This method helps
distinguish these "external" clicks.

An "external" click can be triggered in a number of ways: via a click
on an associated label for a form  associated element, calling
`element.click()`, or calling
`element.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', ...))`.

Also works around Firefox issue
https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1804576 by squelching
events for a microtask after called.

declarations.name: redispatchEvent

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: The element to dispatch the event from.

declarations.description:
Re-dispatches an event from the provided element.

This function is useful for forwarding non-composed events, such as `change`
events.

members.name: variant
members.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

declarations.name: ButtonGroup

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: variant
members.description:
Layout variant of the button group.
`"standard"` shows buttons with a small gap between them.
`"connected"` places buttons with a 2px gap; in horizontal orientation outer buttons have fully
rounded outer corners and middle buttons keep standard rounded corners. In vertical orientation
all buttons keep their default corner shape.
Defaults to `"standard"`.

members.name: orientation
members.description:
Orientation of the button group.
`"horizontal"` lays buttons out in a row.
`"vertical"` stacks buttons in a column.
Defaults to `"horizontal"`.

members.name: color
members.description:
Color applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"primary"`, `"success"`, `"danger"`, `"warning"`, `"light"`, `"dark"`.

members.name: buttonVariant
members.description:
Visual style applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"filled"`, `"tonal"`, `"outlined"`.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Layout variant of the button group.
`"standard"` shows buttons with a small gap between them.
`"connected"` places buttons with a 2px gap; in horizontal orientation outer buttons have fully
rounded outer corners and middle buttons keep standard rounded corners. In vertical orientation
all buttons keep their default corner shape.
Defaults to `"standard"`.

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
Orientation of the button group.
`"horizontal"` lays buttons out in a row.
`"vertical"` stacks buttons in a column.
Defaults to `"horizontal"`.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description:
Color applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"primary"`, `"success"`, `"danger"`, `"warning"`, `"light"`, `"dark"`.

attributes.name: button-variant
attributes.description:
Visual style applied to all buttons in the group.
Possible values are `"filled"`, `"tonal"`, `"outlined"`.

declarations.summary: Group a series of buttons together on a single line with the button group, and super-power.

members.name: type
members.description: Type is preset of color and variant. Type will be only applied.

members.name: variant
members.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

members.name: color
members.description: Defines the primary color of the button. This can be set to predefined color names to apply specific color themes.

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: disabledReason
members.description: If button is disabled, the reason why it is disabled.

members.name: href
members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: throttleDelay
members.description: Sets the delay for throttle in milliseconds. Defaults to 200 milliseconds.

members.name: isPressed
members.description: States

events.description: Dispatched when the button is clicked.
events.name: button:click
